raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a metaphysical limit on human relation to temporality, directing attention away from linear time toward an atemporal present",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "only the present moment can be directly possessed or relinquished",
    "past and future lack independent ontological status as objects of possession",
    "human identity is not constituted by temporal extension"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent within a present-moment or eternal-now ontology but presupposes a non-standard view of time and possession",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the recipient seeks psychological detachment from regret or anticipation and accepts a non-dual or timeless model of consciousness",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the recipient interprets the claim as denying practical value of memory, planning, or historical continuity within ordinary temporal existence"
}
